sections.paragraph:
unused in Kal, to be pained in mind, to be sad. A kindred root is יָגַע.

Piel יִגָּה to grieve, to make sad. Fut. וַיַּגֶּה for וַיְיַגֶּה) Lam 3:33. Compare יָבֵשׁ Piel.

Hiphil הֹוגָה to grieve, to make sad, to afflict, Job 19:2 Lam 1:5, 12 3:32 Isa 51:23.

Niphal נוּגָה (for נֹוגָה), part. afflicted, grieved, sad, Lam 1:4. Zep 3:18, נוּגֵי מִמֹּועֵד “grieved (and excluded) from the holy convocation.”

Derivatives, יָגֹון, תּוּגָה.

II. יָגָה i.q. הָגָה No. II. to be removed.

Hiphil הֹוגָה to remove, 2Sa 20:13. Arab. وجى Conj. IV. id. [“Syr. ܐܘܰܓܺܚ to expel”]. [This passage should be construed, “when he had removed (him);” see Thes.]
